decisive_reason: Wave 1 quarantined stale root and prior F1 audit material before the cleaned lane was rerun, so the retained F1 packet is not a reuse of stale path state.
Default validation stayed exit 1 and explicit-hash validation stayed exit 0 on cleanup and every Wave 2 row, so the capability verdict did not depend on validator-default success or failure.
Wave 1 allocated a fresh audit leaf after quarantine and Wave 2 allocated fresh row-specific audit leaves, so no cited packet reused quarantined output directories.
The raw receipt hashes changed across perturbations, but all six rows normalized to the same semantic digest and all 36 pairwise comparisons report same_semantic_digest=true.
Wave 2 held battery temperature at 34.0 C pre and post on every row with thermal status 0 throughout, so no thermal excursion remains to explain or rescue a split response family.

The cleaned F1 lane still runs through a prebuilt-backed genesis_cli, so hidden internal fallback cannot be ruled out mechanistically, but nothing hidden surfaced as a second retained receipt family under the branch contract.
Wave 2 was explicitly scoped to the cleaned F1 lane only and Wave 5 later rejected any F1-versus-F2 splice as cross-family or unstable, so the retained negative result is not a residue-family mix-up.
Wave 4 could not open a same-family handoff battery because no mapped property existed to carry and no handoff packet was admissible.
Wave 5 found no usable lane because the only accelerator path is cross-family or unstable and speed or hardware presence were explicitly rejected as behavior proxies.
